paper_map.title: Fe–Mn–C–Al Low-Density Steel for Structural Materials: A Review of Alloying, Heat Treatment, Microstructure, and Mechanical Properties
paper_map.abstract_summary: This review focuses on Fe–Mn–C–Al low-density steels, examining how Al, Mn, and C alloying together with subsequent annealing and aging treatments dictate phase constitution (austenite, ferrite, κ-carbide) and mechanical behavior. Key properties covered include work hardening, yield strength, tribological performance, cryogenic impact toughness, and hydrogen embrittlement. The paper summarizes current understanding and identifies challenges for low-density steel design and industrial application.
paper_map.research_objective: To review and discuss the effects of alloying and heat treatment on the microstructure evolution and resulting mechanical properties of Al-containing Fe–Mn–C–Al low-density steel, aiming to provide guidance for high-quality low-density steel design.

key_sections.role: motivation for lightweight structural materials and the development of Fe–Mn–C–Al low-density steel
effects of alloying elements on density, lattice constants, phase composition, and phase diagrams; aging effects on precipitation

key_sections.section: 2.1. Alloying Effect on Density and Lattice Constants
key_sections.role: empirical relationships for density and lattice parameter as functions of Al, Mn, C

key_sections.section: 2.2. Alloying Effect on Phase Composition and Diagram
key_sections.role: phase stability and constitution in Fe–Mn–C–Al system
precipitation of κ-carbide and other phases during aging
work hardening behaviors, yield strength, tribology, cryogenic properties, and hydrogen embrittlement
TWIP, TRIP, SRO, and κ-carbide strengthening mechanisms

key_sections.section: 3.4. Other Mechanical Performance for Engineering Application
key_sections.role: cryogenic temperature properties and hydrogen embrittlement
industrial challenges (MnO, Al-oxides, heat transfer, casting defects) and future directions for low-density steel development
interfacial reactions, mold fluxes, shrinkage issues in high-Mn steels
Al-oxides formation, nozzle clogging, decarburization, surface defects
